agent_notes: Richmond upon Thames has enough quality for 30 picks, but several excellent cultural venues are Tier B because same-category backup within 10 minutes is not available.

Creative Walks were restricted to PAINTS-approved Richmond Park, Kew Gardens and Bushy Park; Marble Hill/Richmond Green were deliberately rejected for Creative Walk use because they are not on the v6 safety list.
Group walk-in and bank-holiday handling are often unpublished; those fields are marked UNVERIFIED where live sources did not prove them.

venues.opening_hours: VisitRichmond lists Mon 12:00-23:00, Tue-Wed 12:00-23:30, Thu 12:00-00:00, Fri-Sat 12:00-00:30, Sun 12:00-22:45; bank holiday/private-hire handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Independent pub format appears walk-in friendly; group of 8-12 seating without splitting is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely workable off-peak for beer-led show-and-tell; call ahead for one table.
venues.capacity_notes: Town-centre pub with food, micro beers and retro soundtrack; avoid Friday/Saturday peak.
venues.why_fits_paints: Independent neighbourhood beer house with a serious draft list gives attendees immediate tasting objects and local-brewer stories without a formal tasting room feel.
venues.anecdote_story: The venue describes itself as Richmond friendly neighbourhood beer house with South West London biggest draft beer selection and local brewers.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Micro beers, gin, cocktails, wine, food menu, retro soundtrack.
venues.safety_notes: Central Richmond, high footfall, close to rail/tube; use lit route via George Street/Kew Road after dark.
venues.backup_venue: Richmond Vault Beer Cellar, about 4 min walk
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 4 min via Hill Street; host should verify live route.
venues.risks_watchouts: Noise/crowding at peak can break conversation; group table policy not published.

venues.opening_hours: Listings conflict: First Table says everyday 12pm till late; Tripadvisor shows Tue-Thu 5pm-11pm and Fri from noon; current hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: OpenTable says not on OpenTable booking network and to contact directly; walk-in group of 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good atmosphere for 8-12 if a quieter vault area can be held; not safe to assume for Friday/Saturday.
venues.capacity_notes: Basement arches/cellar with several nooks; can become music/DJ-led.
venues.why_fits_paints: The underground Georgian-townhouse cellar is visually distinctive and more memorable than a standard Richmond pub, with craft beer as shared object.
venues.anecdote_story: Time Out notes the entrance drops beneath Georgian buildings into arches and passageways, giving the venue its cellar identity.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Craft beer, cocktails, gastropub food, live music/DJ nights on some listings.
venues.safety_notes: Central Richmond and close to station; stairs/cellar layout needs accessibility check.
venues.backup_venue: Tap Tavern, about 4 min walk
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 4 min via Hill Street/Princes Street.
venues.risks_watchouts: Opening-hours inconsistency is the major operational risk; confirm before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Mon-Tue 3pm-8pm, Wed-Fri 3pm-11pm, Sat 12pm-11pm, Sun 12pm-8pm; bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Pub appears walk-in; 8-12 seated together UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Best as a quieter neighbourhood-pub Show & Tell, with host-arranged table.
venues.capacity_notes: Traditional pub beside Richmond Green; children until 5pm note suggests mixed daytime/evening use.
venues.why_fits_paints: A local, unflashy Richmond Green pub gives the category warmth and a strong arrival landmark, useful for strangers finding one another.
venues.anecdote_story: The pub frames itself as Richmond premier watering hole near the Green, with a straightforward community-pub identity.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Beer, wine, spirits, pub food/drinks.
venues.safety_notes: Very close to Richmond Green and station area; avoid late dispersal across unlit park edges.
venues.backup_venue: Tap Tavern, about 6 min walk
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 6 min via Richmond Green/George Street.
venues.risks_watchouts: Less explicitly creative than wine-led venues; group walk-in not published.

venues.opening_hours: VisitRichmond says open all week; Corner listing shows Sat 10am-10pm and updated Apr 2026; exact daily hours/bank holidays UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Wine shop/bar model appears to support drink-in; group of 8-12 walk-in tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong for bottle-label show-and-tell if a tasting/table is agreed in advance.
venues.capacity_notes: Small shop-bar; call ahead and avoid Kew market peak unless intentional.
venues.best_time_slot: Thursday early evening or Sunday after Kew Village Market if confirmed.
venues.why_fits_paints: Independent wine merchant with Kew village context and sustainable/family-producer positioning gives an easy story scaffold for strangers.
venues.anecdote_story: The group has traded for nearly 20 years and describes its focus as good wine, real people and great stories, with family-owned wineries and sustainable, organic or biodynamic standards.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Wine by bottle/glass where available, tastings/events, retail wine expertise.
venues.safety_notes: Kew Village is calm and station-adjacent; late evenings should use the short lit station route.
venues.backup_venue: The Botanist on the Green, about 5 min walk
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 5 min walk around Kew Green; backup is weaker/less independent-feeling but operationally close.
venues.risks_watchouts: If drink-in/table use is not confirmed this becomes retail-only and should not be listed.

venues.venue_name: The Good Wine Shop Richmond Hill
venues.neighbourhood_station: Richmond Hill; Richmond rail/tube by walk/bus
venues.opening_hours: VisitRichmond says The Good Wine Shop branches are open all week apart from some exceptions; exact Richmond Hill branch hours and bank holidays UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Wine merchant/shop-bar model; group drink-in/walk-in policy UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a small wine-led host table if the branch can seat/stand 8-12 comfortably.
venues.capacity_notes: Likely compact retail-led space; host must pre-check layout.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Richmond Hill branch pairs wine conversation with the Turner-view/Richmond Hill story, making a natural Show & Tell prompt.
venues.anecdote_story: Founder Mark Wrigglesworth and the Good Wine Shop model have been profiled in trade press for combining multiple local wine shops around the same service-led approach.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Independent wine merchant selection, sustainable/organic/biodynamic producer focus per partner profile.
venues.safety_notes: Richmond Hill is well-trafficked but steep; define accessible arrival route and station return.

venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 9-10 min downhill toward Richmond Green.

venues.opening_hours: Good Wine Shop/VisitRichmond sources identify the Teddington branch and open-all-week pattern; exact daily hours/bank holidays UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Wine shop/bar use and 8-12 group tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Works if host can arrange a tasting led by the shop or a standing table.

venues.why_fits_paints: Gives Teddington a non-pub drinks option with a clear wine-merchant story and local producer conversation angle.
venues.anecdote_story: The Teddington opening was reported as the fourth branch joining Kew, Richmond Hill and Chiswick; the group is described as award-winning and independent.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Retail wines, possible drink-in/tasting events, sustainable/family-producer wine focus.
venues.safety_notes: Teddington High Street is active and station-linked; use early evening rather than late-night dispersal.
venues.backup_venue: The King’s Head Teddington, about 3 min walk

venues.serve_or_on_view: Wine retail, online/collections, possible tasting/event use if confirmed.

venues.venue_name: Duck Pond Market Richmond, Heron Square
venues.opening_hours: Operator calendar shows Richmond Duck Pond Market 11:00-16:30 on July 2026 dates; Richmond page says all weekend, every weekend. Weather/bank-holiday variations UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market. Meeting point: Kiss the Hippo Richmond or nearby independent coffee within 5 min; group cafe walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent for browsing in pairs and regrouping in Heron Square.
venues.capacity_notes: Weekend artisan food/craft market; Saturday/Sunday stall mix can vary.
venues.why_fits_paints: The strongest Richmond market pick: artisan food and craft in a riverside square, enough texture for show-and-tell objects without needing formal programming.
venues.anecdote_story: Duck Pond says Richmond has hosted the market every weekend since 2010 in Heron Square between Hill Street, Bridge Street and the riverside.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Artisan food, craft, street food, makers, dogs welcome per operator accessibility notes.
venues.safety_notes: High-footfall riverside/town-centre setting; keep winter events to daylight.
venues.backup_venue: Twickenham Farmers Market, about 15 min by transit
venues.backup_walk_time: Market backup allowed by 15 min transit rule; verify live train/bus.
venues.risks_watchouts: Outdoor weather and variable stall count; cafe meeting point must be confirmed.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Holly Road car park; Twickenham rail
venues.opening_hours: London Farmers Markets lists every Saturday 9am-1pm; VisitRichmond lists 2025-2026 Saturday dates. Bank-holiday/weather exceptions UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market. Meeting point: independent coffee on Church Street/King Street within 5 min; exact 8-12 walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good producer-led morning format for 8-12 with station-adjacent meeting.
venues.capacity_notes: Car-park market, functional rather than romantic; producer quality carries the score.
venues.why_fits_paints: A reliable weekly farmers market with food producers gives Richmond borough a practical Market Day outside the obvious riverside cluster.
venues.anecdote_story: Listed by London Farmers Markets as a weekly Saturday farmers market behind Marks & Spencer/Holly Road.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Farm produce and food stalls; exact weekly traders vary.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime central Twickenham, close to station; straightforward dispersal.
venues.backup_venue: Duck Pond Market Richmond, about 15 min by rail/bus
venues.backup_walk_time: Market backup allowed by 15 min transit rule; verify live transit.
venues.risks_watchouts: Less aesthetic than Heron Square; weather and stall mix may affect vibe.

venues.opening_hours: Official FAQ says first Sunday of every month except January, 10am-2pm; bank-holiday impact UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market. Meeting point: independent Kew cafe within 5 min; 8-12 cafe walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent for 8-12, with natural split-and-regroup flow around station parades.
venues.capacity_notes: Around 48 stalls, street closures, live music, volunteer-run.
venues.why_fits_paints: The most PAINTS-coded market in the borough: volunteer-run, maker/food mix, village streets, live music, and proximity to Kew Gardens as cultural anchor.
venues.anecdote_story: Official sources say operating profits go to local charities and the market has donated over GBP108,000 since launch.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Food, crafts, street food, local bands, charity/community stall.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime, station-front village; avoid using non-approved green spaces as meeting points.
venues.backup_venue: Duck Pond Market Richmond, about 10-15 min by District/Overground or bus
venues.backup_walk_time: Market backup allowed by 15 min transit rule; verify live service.
venues.risks_watchouts: Monthly only; January excluded; poor weather can thin dwell time.

venues.opening_hours: VisitRichmond and market social sources list every Saturday 8:30am-2pm; bank-holiday/weather exceptions UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market. Meeting point: independent Barnes cafe within 5 min; group cafe walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 morning browsers; village layout is calm and legible.
venues.capacity_notes: Around 25 stalls per Instagram/listings; one of the older London farmers markets per official site.
venues.why_fits_paints: Barnes gives PAINTS a village-feeling producer market with enough quality food detail for relaxed stranger conversation.
venues.anecdote_story: Official site describes it as one of London oldest farmers markets and recently awarded 2nd best farmers market in London/8th in the UK.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Organic/free-range meats, fish, fruit and veg, bakery, cheese, juices and weekly producers.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime Barnes village; station walk is calm but host should define route.
venues.backup_venue: Kew Village Market when date aligns, otherwise Duck Pond Richmond by transit
venues.backup_walk_time: No same-day 15 min walk backup; closest qualifying market backup is Kew/Richmond by short transit depending date.

venues.opening_hours: VisitRichmond lists 2026 dates including 25 Jul, 29 Aug, 26 Sep at 09:30; Instagram says last Saturday monthly 9:30-1:30. Host must confirm current date/time.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market. Meeting point: Sanctuary Coffee/High Street independent cafe nearby; 8-12 walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for Teddington residents/visitors; compact enough for a hosted loop.
venues.capacity_notes: Monthly food market at/near The King’s Head, championing local, authentic, small-batch produce.
venues.why_fits_paints: Small-batch produce in a neighbourhood setting gives PAINTS a quieter, less obvious Market Day with a real local-trader feel.
venues.anecdote_story: Ted’s Market describes itself as a monthly food market championing local, authentic and small-batch produce.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Local/seasonal food produce and prepared foods; exact traders vary.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime High Street; station nearby.
venues.backup_venue: Twickenham Farmers Market, about 15 min by bus/rail or short taxi
venues.backup_walk_time: Market backup allowed by 15 min transit rule; verify live transit.

venues.venue_name: Richmond Park: Pembroke Lodge, King Henry’s Mound and Isabella Plantation loop
venues.neighbourhood_station: Richmond Park/Richmond Hill; Richmond rail/tube by bus/walk
venues.opening_hours: Royal Parks says pedestrian gates 24/7 and vehicle gates 7am-9pm; Isabella Plantation/gardens can have separate/seasonal access, so route closures UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access park route; no venue walk-in needed. Weather backup within 5 min start: Pembroke Lodge cafe, group capacity UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent for 8-12 daytime walkers; keep to compact loop and regroup at each stop.
venues.capacity_notes: Wide paths around lodge/viewpoint; Isabella paths narrower, split briefly if needed.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday/Sunday 10:30am-12:30pm or weekday golden-hour summer, not after dark.
venues.why_fits_paints: Premium Launch approved park with one of London best viewpoint stories, deer-park ecology and a contained route that lets strangers talk while moving.
venues.anecdote_story: Stops: Pembroke Lodge; King Henry’s Mound protected St Paul’s view; Isabella Plantation Victorian woodland garden; deer/ancient landscape shaped over 700 years.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Landscape, protected view, woodland garden, deer ecology; cafe backup at Pembroke Lodge.
venues.safety_notes: PAINTS-approved Premium Launch park. Daylight only recommended; keep 50m from deer, avoid rutting season close contact and isolated late exits.
venues.backup_venue: Pembroke Lodge cafe, within 5 min of start
venues.backup_walk_time: Within 5 min weather/safety backup; host must confirm seating for 8-12.
venues.risks_watchouts: Obviousness penalty for iconic park; deer/seasonal gate notices must be briefed.

venues.venue_name: Richmond Park: Ham Gate to Isabella Plantation art-and-ecology walk
venues.opening_hours: Royal Parks pedestrian gates 24/7; vehicle gates 7am-9pm; Isabella Plantation access/temporary closures UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access route. Weather backup within 5 min start is limited; Ham cafes/pubs outside gate need host confirmation.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 active walkers; use daylight and a clear gate meeting point.
venues.capacity_notes: Paths vary; keep group together at crossings and plantation entrances.
venues.why_fits_paints: A quieter Richmond Park route with less tourist pressure than Richmond Gate, strong botanical texture and a clean PAINTS safety-list fit.
venues.anecdote_story: Stops: Ham Gate approach; Ham Cross; Isabella Plantation 40-acre Victorian woodland garden; Broomfield Hill/Pen Ponds return if extended.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Woodland garden, wetland/pond ecology, seasonal flowering, deer-park context.
venues.safety_notes: Premium Launch approved park; daylight only, avoid isolated dusk route and keep deer distance.
venues.backup_venue: No qualifying indoor backup found within 5 min of Ham Gate; closest likely Ham Parade cafe/pub approx. 10-12 min
venues.backup_walk_time: No qualifying weather backup within 5 min; demoted to Tier B.
venues.risks_watchouts: Backup constraint is the weakness; meet at Ham Gate only in good weather.

venues.venue_name: Kew Gardens: Palm House, Temperate House, Marianne North and Great Pagoda route
venues.opening_hours: Kew lists 1 May-31 Aug 2026 Mon-Fri 10am-7pm last entry 6pm, weekends/bank holidays 10am-8pm last entry 7pm; attractions have separate closures, host must check planned closures.
venues.walk_in_policy: Ticketed garden; same-day entry often possible but group ticket/walk-in availability UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent for 8-12 with pre-bought tickets and timed meeting at Victoria Gate.
venues.capacity_notes: Large site; stops can absorb group, but glasshouses can bottleneck.
venues.best_time_slot: Weekday 3pm-5:30pm or Sunday late morning outside peak queues.
venues.why_fits_paints: Premium Launch approved garden with built-in art, plant science and architecture. It functions as a walk and a show-and-tell museum without needing extra programming.
venues.anecdote_story: Stops: Palm House Victorian glasshouse; Temperate House; Marianne North’s 833-painting gallery; Great Pagoda 1762 landmark.
venues.serve_or_on_view: World Heritage botanic landscape, glasshouses, botanical art, Great Pagoda, galleries.
venues.safety_notes: PAINTS-approved Premium Launch park/garden. High staff/visitor presence; ticketed controlled environment.
venues.backup_venue: The Original Maids of Honour / Kew Green cafes, about 5 min from Victoria Gate
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 5 min from Victoria Gate; host must confirm 8-12 seating.
venues.risks_watchouts: Ticketing and planned closures; Palm House renovation begins 2027 but host should check if any early works affect route.

venues.venue_name: Bushy Park: Diana Fountain, Woodland Gardens and Longford River walk
venues.opening_hours: Royal Parks/Bushy FAQ: pedestrian gates usually 24 hours except deer cull periods; vehicle gates open from 6:30am and close at dusk; Woodland Gardens gates around 09:00/09:30 to dusk; host must check notices.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access park route. Weather backup near start: Pheasantry Cafe/nearby Hampton Court cafes; group seating UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 in daylight; use short route and precise meeting point.
venues.capacity_notes: Wide park avenues, but garden areas narrower.
venues.why_fits_paints: Premium Launch approved park with deer, water, royal engineering and a quieter west-London texture than Kew/Richmond Park.
venues.anecdote_story: Stops: Diana Fountain; Longford River engineered watercourse; Woodland Gardens; deer landscape linked to Hampton Court.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Royal park landscape, waterways, deer, historic fountain/gardens.
venues.safety_notes: PAINTS-approved Premium Launch park. Daylight only; deer cull periods and dusk gate changes require checks.
venues.backup_venue: Pheasantry Cafe / Hampton Court village cafes, within about 5 min depending start
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 5 min from Diana Fountain/Pheasantry side; host must confirm.
venues.risks_watchouts: Part of Bushy/Hampton Court orbit can feel boundary-adjacent; deer notices and garden opening times matter.

venues.venue_name: Richmond Station to Ham House historic scenic walk
venues.neighbourhood_station: Richmond riverside to Ham; Richmond rail/tube
venues.opening_hours: National Trust route is open walking route; Ham House has separate opening and ticketing. Route daylight/weather conditions UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access urban/river route, but includes non-park Thames/riverside sections; weather backup near start: Richmond town centre cafes within 5 min, group seating UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for fit walkers; 4.5 mile route may be long for PAINTS default unless shortened.
venues.capacity_notes: Public paths; split into conversation pairs and reconvene at viewpoints.
venues.best_time_slot: Sunday 10am-12:30pm shortened route, or summer early afternoon.
venues.why_fits_paints: Official National Trust route has high story value linking Richmond station, Richmond Hill views and Ham House, but it is less cleanly within the Creative Walk safety list than park-only routes.
venues.anecdote_story: Stops: Richmond station/town edge; Richmond Hill protected view; Thames towpath; Ham House approach.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Urban greens, Thames views, Ham House exterior/setting, Richmond Hill art-history cue.
venues.safety_notes: Uses approved Richmond Park-adjacent geography but also riverside public paths; daylight only and host should pre-walk.
venues.backup_venue: Richmond town centre cafe/pub, within 5 min of start
venues.backup_walk_time: Within 5 min from Richmond station start; exact venue UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.risks_watchouts: Because non-park route is not one of the explicitly approved canal routes, treat as Tier B and seek PAINTS team review before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: VisitRichmond says free to visit and directs to official site for current details; exact current hours, exhibition dates and bank holidays UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.

venues.why_fits_paints: The borough’s most natural PAINTS gallery pick: public art, riverside setting, and enough architectural character to make a slow looking session work.
venues.anecdote_story: The Octagon Room and Orleans House history give a strong object-story anchor beyond the current exhibitions.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Exhibitions, gallery collection/programming, historic Octagon Room.
venues.safety_notes: Daylight riverside route preferred; station walk should be planned as a group after dark.

venues.opening_hours: VisitRichmond lists Thu-Sun 12:00-18:00, last admission 17:30; Mon-Wed appointments only; group times outside these available on request.
venues.walk_in_policy: Museum appears walk-in during public hours; group of 8-12 should be confirmed, especially small museum capacity.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent for a music-history show-and-tell group if capacity confirmed.
venues.capacity_notes: Small museum; staggered looking may be needed.

venues.anecdote_story: Eel Pie Island’s music mythology includes jazz, blues and rock heritage tied to Twickenham’s riverside scene.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Music memorabilia, island/local history, exhibitions on Eel Pie Island culture.

venues.backup_venue: Orleans House Gallery, about 10 min walk
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 10 min via Church Street/riverside.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Richmond Old Town Hall; Richmond rail/tube
venues.opening_hours: Art Fund lists Tue-Fri 11am-5pm and Sat 10am-4pm; bank holidays/exhibition changes UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free museum; 8-12 group walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm because museum is compact.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a quieter local-history group; pair with riverside discussion after.
venues.capacity_notes: Small museum inside Old Town Hall; avoid school/group conflict times.
venues.why_fits_paints: Not spectacular, but highly useful for Richmond context: local objects, town history and a civic building make it a grounded PAINTS stop.
venues.anecdote_story: The museum sits in the Old Town Hall and interprets Richmond borough history; strong as an orientation before walking to riverside/market.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Local history displays and temporary exhibitions.
venues.safety_notes: Central, high-footfall, station-adjacent; straightforward daytime use.
venues.backup_venue: Orleans House Gallery is outside 10 min walk; closest cultural backup is Riverside Gallery/Old Town Hall if open, otherwise no qualifying same-category backup.

venues.opening_hours: London Luminaries lists house daily 12pm-4pm and garden daily 10am-5pm/seasonal; National Trust live page must be checked for 2026 opening, closures and tickets.
venues.walk_in_policy: National Trust property; walk-in may depend on ticket capacity and room flow. Group 8-12 admission UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong for 8-12 if tickets/timed entry arranged; rooms can be narrow.
venues.capacity_notes: Historic house with garden; group may need to move in pairs through rooms.
venues.why_fits_paints: Atmospheric, object-rich and quietly strange: a 17th-century house whose textiles, furniture and river setting give endless prompts.
venues.anecdote_story: VisitRichmond describes Ham House as virtually unchanged for 400 years and internationally recognised for textiles, furniture and art kept in the house for centuries.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Historic interiors, art/furniture/textiles, formal gardens, cafe/shop.
venues.safety_notes: Daylight arrival by bus/walk recommended; riverside route beautiful but avoid isolated dusk use.
venues.backup_venue: Orleans House Gallery / Eel Pie Island Museum are over 10 min walk; no qualifying same-category backup within 10 min.
venues.backup_walk_time: No qualifying same-category backup within 10 min; closest cultural alternatives in Twickenham/Richmond by bus/walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: No tight backup and ticket/live opening checks required, so Tier B despite high cultural fit.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists House & Shop Wed-Sun 10am-5pm, last entry 4pm; FAQ says open including Bank Holidays and 7 days in school holidays; host must confirm target date.
venues.walk_in_policy: Ticketed house; same-day tickets may exist but group 8-12 walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must book/check.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent for 8-12 with timed tickets; cafe/garden can support post-look discussion.
venues.capacity_notes: Historic rooms; manage pace to avoid crowding.
venues.why_fits_paints: A first-rate PAINTS object: theatrical Gothic revival, literary invention, and a house that was shown to visitors in Walpole’s own day.
venues.anecdote_story: Official history says Horace Walpole allowed four visitors a day with rules; the FT described Strawberry Hill as both first Gothic Revival house and linked to the first Gothic novel.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Historic house, Gothic interiors, garden and cafe, exhibitions/events.
venues.safety_notes: Short walk from Strawberry Hill station; daylight route recommended.
venues.backup_venue: Turner’s House, about 20 min walk / short bus, not within 10 min
venues.backup_walk_time: No qualifying same-category backup within 10 min; closest strong backup is Turner’s House beyond radius.
venues.risks_watchouts: No tight backup, paid/timed entry, and occasional early closures.

venues.opening_hours: Official homepage says Wed-Sun 12pm-4pm with guided tours 12:30, 1:30 and 2:30; plan page notes exceptional closure 15 July 2026; host must confirm target date.
venues.walk_in_policy: Small ticketed house; recommends booking because visitor numbers are restricted. Group walk-in not suitable without confirmation.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 only with booking/private tour; otherwise capacity-limited.
venues.capacity_notes: Small villa; private tours up to 15 noted by official site.
venues.best_time_slot: Wednesday-Friday 12:30pm guided tour or private tour.
venues.why_fits_paints: A compact, high-story artist house: J.M.W. Turner designed and built his own retreat, ideal for slow-looking and creative prompts.
venues.anecdote_story: Official history states Turner built Sandycombe Lodge in 1813 as a small villa near the Thames at Twickenham.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Artist house, guided tours, talks, exhibitions/events on Turner’s art and legacy.
venues.safety_notes: Residential St Margarets; good daylight station route.
venues.backup_venue: Orleans House Gallery, approx. 20 min walk; no same-category backup within 10 min.
venues.backup_walk_time: No qualifying same-category backup within 10 min; closest strong cultural backup beyond radius.
venues.risks_watchouts: Booking recommended/restricted numbers demote operational robustness.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Kew Gardens; Kew Gardens station/Victoria Gate
venues.opening_hours: Kew galleries listed 10am-5:30pm last entry 5pm; Marianne North page says check planned closures, with reopening/closure notices; host must check live Kew planned closures.
venues.walk_in_policy: Included with Kew Gardens ticket; group entry depends on garden admission/ticketing. Host must confirm 8-12 tickets.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent for slow-looking with a maximum 8-12; gallery walls are dense and conversation-rich.
venues.capacity_notes: Gallery can handle visitors but group should not block narrow viewing areas.
venues.why_fits_paints: A near-perfect PAINTS gallery: 833 paintings by a Victorian woman artist-traveller displayed with almost overwhelming intensity.
venues.anecdote_story: Kew brochure notes Marianne North chose to display 833 paintings geographically after travelling around the world.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Botanical paintings, Kew Gardens context, nearby Shirley Sherwood Gallery.
venues.safety_notes: Staffed ticketed garden; use Victoria Gate and daylight/evening opening as posted.
venues.backup_venue: Shirley Sherwood Gallery, about 1 min walk
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 1 min next door within Kew Gardens.
venues.risks_watchouts: Requires Kew admission and closure checks; obviousness penalty for Kew brand.

venues.venue_name: Shirley Sherwood Gallery of Botanical Art, Kew Gardens
venues.neighbourhood_station: Kew Gardens; Kew Gardens station/Victoria Gate
venues.opening_hours: Kew lists Shirley Sherwood Gallery 10am-5:30pm last entry 5pm; planned closures page must be checked before visit.
venues.walk_in_policy: Included with Kew Gardens admission; 8-12 tickets/group entry UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong for 8-12, especially paired with Marianne North next door.
venues.capacity_notes: Gallery scale is suitable but current exhibitions can draw queues.
venues.best_time_slot: Weekday late morning or late afternoon.
venues.why_fits_paints: The world’s first public gallery dedicated to classic and contemporary botanical art is a strong PAINTS fit for art, nature and close looking.
venues.anecdote_story: Kew says the gallery has hosted almost 60 exhibitions since opening in 2008, showing botanical art from Kew’s archive and the Shirley Sherwood collection.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Classic and contemporary botanical art, Kew archive works, changing exhibitions.
venues.safety_notes: Controlled garden environment with staff and clear exits.
venues.backup_venue: Marianne North Gallery, about 1 min walk
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 1 min next door within Kew Gardens.
venues.risks_watchouts: Kew ticket and planned closures; current exhibition content must be checked.

venues.opening_hours: VisitRichmond lists Mon-Sat 10:00-18:00, Sun 10:00-17:00; bank holiday handling UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookshop walk-in normal; 8-12 browse-and-talk tolerated only if host confirms. Coffee pairing nearby UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Excellent for book/record prompts; discuss outside or at paired cafe after browsing.
venues.capacity_notes: Small curated shop with events space; 8-12 should be arranged.
venues.why_fits_paints: The most PAINTS-coded bookshop in Richmond: independent, diverse/multicultural curation, records, events and an arts-publishing backstory.
venues.anecdote_story: SW Londoner reported the shop’s first birthday, events from poetry slams to author talks, and links to Aurora Metro/Supernova and the Virginia Woolf statue fundraising.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Curated books, records, events, poetry readings, arts/community programming.
venues.safety_notes: Hill Rise is active and near Richmond station; steep hill/accessibility note.
venues.backup_venue: The Open Book, about 8 min walk
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8 min down Hill Rise/King Street.
venues.risks_watchouts: Coffee element is paired rather than built-in; confirm group/cafe plan.

venues.opening_hours: Official/Wix page says Mon-Sat 9:30am-5:30pm and Sun 11am-5pm; Hive lists similar longer hours. Bank holidays UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Independent bookshop walk-in; 8-12 browsing/group discussion UNVERIFIED - host must confirm; coffee pairing nearby required.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for browsing prompts then nearby cafe; shop is long/narrow.
venues.capacity_notes: Bookshop.org describes it as about 3 metres wide and 22 metres deep, so avoid blocking aisles.
venues.why_fits_paints: A proper old Richmond independent: small frontage, deep shelves, charm, signed local-literary stock and enough oddness to make browsing social.
venues.anecdote_story: The shop says it has been independent in Richmond since 1987; Bookshop.org says it has around 25,000 books and often signed books by local literary figures.
venues.serve_or_on_view: New/second-hand books, cards, events/orders; paired coffee nearby.
venues.safety_notes: Central Richmond; very easy station route.
venues.backup_venue: The Alligator’s Mouth, about 3 min walk
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 3 min via King Street/Church Court.
venues.risks_watchouts: Narrow shop and no in-house coffee; host must manage flow.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Mon-Fri 10am-5:30pm, Sat 10am-6pm, Sun 11am-4pm, Bank Holidays 11am-4pm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookshop walk-in normal; 8-12 group browse/discussion UNVERIFIED - host must confirm. Coffee pairing nearby required.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Best for children’s/illustration-led PAINTS prompts or nostalgic object sharing; group should browse in small clusters.
venues.capacity_notes: Small specialist shop; confirm before bringing 8-12 adults.
venues.why_fits_paints: A specialist children’s bookshop can be surprisingly adult-rich for PAINTS: memory, illustration, first books, school stories and biscuits-on-request warmth.
venues.anecdote_story: The shop quotes Lemony Snicket on its name and describes itself as independent children’s bookshop with expert staff, events, author signings, subscriptions and biscuits on request.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Children’s/YA books, cards, board games, events, author signings; paired coffee nearby.
venues.safety_notes: Central Richmond pedestrian court; easy transit and backup bookshops nearby.
venues.backup_venue: The Open Book, about 3 min walk
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 3 min walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Category skews children’s; host should frame prompts carefully for adult strangers.

venues.opening_hours: VisitRichmond notes closures on Easter Sunday, Christmas Day and Boxing Day; exact weekly hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm with Hewson Books.
venues.walk_in_policy: Independent bookshop walk-in; 8-12 group/cafe pairing UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 if paired with Kew cafe/market; keep browsing small-group.
venues.capacity_notes: Small village bookshop; group should not block entrance, especially market Sundays.
venues.best_time_slot: First Sunday after Kew Village Market or weekday late morning.
venues.why_fits_paints: Leafy Kew village, station-adjacent, genuinely local independent, and easy to pair with market/Kew Gardens without becoming a chain-bookshop event.
venues.anecdote_story: Hewson Books says The Kew Bookshop and The Sheen Bookshop are local independent booksellers hosting book events, talks, launches and signings.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Books, stationery, gift wrap, tote bags, events/talks via Hewson Books.
venues.safety_notes: Station-front village; safe daylight and early evening.
venues.backup_venue: Lloyds of Kew Bookshop, about 3 min walk
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 3 min via Station Approach/Mortlake Terrace.
venues.risks_watchouts: Current hours not fully published on snippet; confirm before listing.

venues.opening_hours: Official site shows open today 10am-4pm but notes variable June hours/closures; current weekly hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Second-hand bookshop walk-in; group of 8-12 and coffee pairing UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good as a browsing backup or small treasure-hunt prompt; less robust as primary.
venues.capacity_notes: Second-hand bookshop; likely compact and browsing-led.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday/Sunday late morning if open confirmed.
venues.why_fits_paints: Independent second-hand bookshop near Kew Gardens gives PAINTS a slower, object-hunt version of Books & Coffee.
venues.anecdote_story: The shop presents itself as a general independent second-hand bookshop in Kew, with changing stock and cards.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Second-hand books, cards, browsing finds; paired coffee nearby.
venues.safety_notes: Kew village/station area; straightforward daylight use.
venues.backup_venue: The Kew Bookshop, about 3 min walk
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 3 min walk.
venues.risks_watchouts: Variable opening hours make this Tier B; host must confirm live status.

venues.opening_hours: Hewson Books identifies The Sheen Bookshop; exact weekly hours/bank holidays UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Independent bookshop walk-in; group 8-12 and coffee pairing UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for neighbourhood book browsing with nearby cafe discussion.
venues.capacity_notes: Small local bookshop; arrange visit ahead.
venues.why_fits_paints: Useful East Sheen counterweight to Richmond/Kew cluster and part of an established local independent bookseller pair.
venues.anecdote_story: Hewson Books says it provides a friendly, relaxed atmosphere, community bookselling and events/talks/signings through its Kew and Sheen shops.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Books, events/talks/signings via Hewson Books; paired coffee nearby.
venues.safety_notes: Upper Richmond Road is busy and well served; use daylight/early evening.

venues.opening_hours: VisitRichmond lists the address; third-party listings show Mon-Sat about 9:30am-5:30pm. Official current hours/bank holidays UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Independent bookshop walk-in; 8-12 group and coffee pairing UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.

venues.capacity_notes: Village bookshop, likely compact; split browsing in pairs.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday after Barnes Farmers Market, around 11:30am-1pm.
venues.why_fits_paints: Barnes village gives this a calm, literary-neighbourhood feel and pairs naturally with the farmers market for a low-pressure morning.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Marble Hill House / Marble Hill Park
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Marble Hill is culturally strong but the park is not on the PAINTS v6 approved Creative Walk safety list; do not source as Creative Walk until team review. It can remain a cultural context note for Orleans House/Twickenham only.
Attractive and central, but not on the PAINTS approved park/route safety list for Creative Walks. Used only as vicinity context for nearby pubs, not as a sourced walk.
Characterful independent bar/restaurant with music mentions, but not enough current official operational evidence for 8-12 walk-in drinks and same-category backup against PAINTS gates.
Rejected because it is a chain bar; PAINTS Show & Tell Drinks requires independent wine bar/pub/gastro pub with owner story.
Rejected as a chain bookshop when Richmond has multiple independent bookshops that better fit PAINTS taste criteria.
Independent wine merchant with promising curation, but public evidence supports retail/delivery more than drink-in group hosting; backup and walk-in gates not met.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Hampton Court Palace Food Festival / artisan food market days
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Removed from included venues because Hampton Court Palace is boundary-adjacent/East Molesey rather than clearly in the London Borough of Richmond upon Thames; PAINTS district output should not stretch geography for volume.
